title: Why Are So Many Americans in Prison? By Steven Raphael and Michael A. Stoll Russell Sage Foundation. 2013. 336 pages. $45 paperback
abstract: The book under review is a compilation of work that was completed over many years. Some of the work was done when Steven Raphael and Michael Stoll were part of a Russell Sage Foundation working group on mass incarceration. Bushway was also part of the working group, and he is acknowledged in the book for “feedback over the years.” Most everyone who reads Social Forces knows that the American incarceration rate has increased fourfold over the past 40 years, leaving the United States with the highest incarceration rate in the world. What we do not know, somewhat surprisingly, is why. Economists Steve Raphael and Michael Stoll approach this question in their book with commendable analytical rigor. After first describing the current rates of incarceration in the United States, they explore possible explanations, including changes in sentencing policy, the deinstitutionalization of the mentally ill, demographic change, economic change, and the crack epidemic.
authors_text: Washington, Heather M.; Bushway, Shawn D.
